name: Donald J. Trump for President, Inc.
subject: Join Us in Richmond, VA Today!
body:
| | [](http://donaldtrump.nationbuilder.com/?e=8ff4c67fd343da79b63c0de3e94ed779&utm_source=donaldtrump&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=richmond1&n=1)
---
# [www.DonaldJTrump.com ](//www.donaldjtrump.com)
---
|
---
\- JUNE 10th 2016 -
JOIN US IN RICHMOND, VA TODAY!
| Dear Peter,

We are excited to announce that Donald J. Trump and team are headed to Richmond, Virginia today, June 10th, for a Donald J. Trump for President rally and we want you to join us! Details about the event are below:

**Friday, June 10th: Richmond, VA
** Richmond Coliseum
601 East Leigh Street
Richmond, VA 23219 Doors Open: 5:00 PM
Event Begins: 8:00 PM
Register for tickets by [clicking here](https://donaldtrump.nationbuilder.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.donaldjtrump.com%2Fschedule%2Fregister%2Frichmond-va%2F&e=8ff4c67fd343da79b63c0de3e94ed779&utm_source=donaldtrump&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=richmond1&n=2).

Please limit personal items and arrive early to expedite entrance into the venue - please note, **NO homemade signs, banners, professional cameras with a detachable lens, tripods, monopods, selfie sticks, back packs or large bags will be permitted into the venue.**

We hope to see you there! Sincerely,

Team Trump

* * *

TEXT **TRUMP** TO **88022** TO GET EXCLUSIVE UPDATES ***By participating, you consent to receiving automated text messages from the campaign. Standard messaging rates apply.***

[Click Here ](https://donaldtrump.nationbuilder.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.donaldjtrump.com%2Fdonate%2F&e=8ff4c67fd343da79b63c0de3e94ed779&utm_source=donaldtrump&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=richmond1&n=3)To Stand With Donald Trump and
MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN!

P.S. If you would like to purchase official campaign gear, [click here](https://donaldtrump.nationbuilder.com/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fshop.donaldjtrump.com&e=8ff4c67fd343da79b63c0de3e94ed779&utm_source=donaldtrump&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=richmond1&n=4).

name: staff@maggiehassan.com
subject: Link of the Week: (The Notorious) Ruth Bader Ginsburg
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
_This is our Link of the Week email -- the things that we're sharing around the office, and we think everyone on Team Maggie should know about. We hope you'll enjoy clicking around as much as we do._
The Supreme Court isn't meant to function without nine justices -- just ask Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg (or as the Internet commonly calls her, Notorious R.B.G.):
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd480/2a380d31/139452bb/f82d2c/3543078067/VEsH/ "http://www.cnn.com/2016/05/26/politics/ruth-bader-ginsburg-eight-justices/")
But it's what we've got since Kelly Ayotte, Mitch McConnell, and other Washington Republicans refuse to do their job. You can read Justice Ginsburg's full comments on why the current SCOTUS situation is notoriously bad [**here.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd480/2a380d31/139452bb/f82d2c/3543078067/VEsE/ "http://www.cnn.com/2016/05/26/politics/ruth-bader-ginsburg-eight-justices/")
--Team Maggie
P.S. -- Seriously though, it's ridiculous that Kelly Ayotte and her fellow GOP Senators still won't bother to move the Supreme Court confirmation process forward. [**If you want the next Senator from New Hampshire to actually do her job, chip in today to elect Maggie Hassan and secure the future of the Supreme Court.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd480/2a380d31/139452bb/f82d2d/3543078067/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiaGFzc2FuX3Nlbl9lbWFpbCIsImUzc2djbVZtWTI5a1pTQjlmUT09IjoiZTIwMTYwNjEwX2xvdHciLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/hassan_sen_email?refcode=e20160610_lotw&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500")

[Contribute](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd480/2a380d31/139452bb/f82d2d/3543078067/VEsC/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiaGFzc2FuX3Nlbl9lbWFpbCIsImUzc2djbVZtWTI5a1pTQjlmUT09IjoiZTIwMTYwNjEwX2xvdHciLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/hassan_sen_email?refcode=e20160610_lotw&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500")

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
To unsubscribe from the Maggie for NH email list, please click here: [Unsubscribe](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd480/2a380d31/139452bb/f82d2e/3543078067/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")

Paid for by Maggie for NH

Contributions or gifts to Maggie for NH are not tax deductible.

Maggie for NH
PO Box 298
Concord, NH 03302 United States

name: =?utf-8?Q?Team=20Scott?=
subject: Your Weekly Update
body:
| | | Week in Action
---
[View this email in your browser](http://us13.campaign-archive1.com/?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=46c5efc6c1&e=19cebfe173)
---
| |
---
| Greetings,
---
| This week Phil continued to meet with Vermonters and tour Vermont businesses, while also introducing new policy proposals, and tackling issues back at the statehouse. It was a busy week that, as usual, saw Phil rolling up his sleeves and working hard for Vermonters. A few of this week’s highlights from the state’s most energetic and hardworking campaign are below.

**PHIL INTRODUCES PLAN FOR GOVERNMENT MODERNIZATION**

Our state government is functioning on outdated systems that no longer meet the everyday needs and expectations of 21st century citizens. As Phil said this week, **we need to modernize, and where necessary, restructure government by integrating services for Vermonters to improve their experience.** The state's disastrous and expensive experience with Vermont Health Connect has taught us we need to have far better project management protocols in place.

Recognizing these challenges, Phil introduced a plan detailing how he’ll work to **modernize state government by _streamlining services_ , implementing _proven protocols_ and systems, leveraging _experienced IT and project management professionals_ , _empowering state employees_ and _establishing metrics_ and a committee to measure and ensure results.**

View Phil’s plan to modernize state government, creating a path for efficiency through innovation, [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=5185862be5&e=19cebfe173).
***
**FIGHTING TO KEEP WORKERS HERE IN VERMONT**
We were all discouraged to hear the news of 108 layoffs at Keurig, and our hearts go out to the impacted Vermont workers and their families. Phil learned from Keurig that it will do everything in its power to place those employees in new positions within the company. Further, **Phil is committed to work in conjunction with State leaders, agencies and the impacted employees to get them back to work, and keep them here in Vermont.**

These layoffs are a sad reminder of the critical need to improve our economy so businesses can innovate and thrive in Vermont. Phil continues to fight to ensure the economy and affordability remain the top issues of discussion during the campaign, because they will be his top priorities as Governor.

Read his official statement on the layoffs [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=dd411a1e45&e=19cebfe173) and learn more about his approach to strengthening our economy and making Vermont more affordable for working families and businesses [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=dca981fbc0&e=19cebfe173).
***
**ADDRESSING CRITICAL ISSUES...**

**...AT VERMONT FORUMS**
Phil had the opportunity to share his views and plans to tackle some of Vermont’s challenges and capitalize on its opportunities at multiple Gubernatorial forums and group discussions this week. He shared his vision for strengthening the economy for businesses at the **Vermont Business Roundtable Forum** , and discussed the importance of responsibly addressing our water quality challenges at the **Clean Water Economy Forum** and the **Water Quality Conference**. He also honored local fire fighters at the **Professional Fire Fighters of Vermont Convention** and joined the Orleans County GOP for its **“Honor the Vets” event** , a cause that’s very near to Phil’s heart. Read his statement in honor of D-Day [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=91ff285335&e=19cebfe173).

Photo Credit: @PhilScott4VT (except where otherwise noted)
**...AND DIRECTLY WITH VERMONTERS**
Team Scott continued meeting with Vermonters throughout the state, with additional stops in Burlington, Chittenden County, East Bethel’s Integrity Energy, Enosburg (for its wonderful Dairy Days parade) and Ferrisburg. Thanks to everyone for sharing your stories and reinforcing why we love Vermont – and why we must elect Phil as Governor to help move our state forward.

Photo Credit: @PhilScott4VT
Want to connect with Phil at an upcoming event? Next week we’ll be cruising into Addison County for the [Addison County Chamber Forum](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=ca19baf860&e=19cebfe173), and making new stops in [Manchester](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=701eebfc5c&e=19cebfe173) and [Barre](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=2637664a49&e=19cebfe173) – we hope to see many of you there. Check out our full listing of events [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=98bfffe60e&e=19cebfe173).
***
**ICYMI: PHIL IN THE NEWS**

**_“I support Scott for Governor”_**
**“** Phil is a businessman, a volunteer entrepreneur with Wheels for Warmth, a state senator and lieutenant governor. Of all the candidates, he has the vision, commitment, personal make-up, and experience that best fits this challenging work.” – Paul Carroccio, Manchester (Source: Brattleboro Reformer: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=0077d89725&e=19cebfe173))

**_Phil: Make sure Vermonters have a say_**
“Lt. Gov. Phil Scott, a Republican candidate for governor, sent a letter Monday to legislators arguing that any new [energy siting] bill in the works should be disclosed immediately so the public can weigh in. ‘Otherwise ... I would recommend you override the governor’s veto,’ he wrote.” (Source: Seven Days: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=17c0d8208a&e=19cebfe173))

**_Phil: Let’s help Vermonters following Keurig layoffs_**
“For my part, I will work in conjunction with State leaders, agencies and the employees themselves to get them back to work here in Vermont." (Source: WPTZ: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=86758d0b19&e=19cebfe173))
***
**ICYMI: THIS WEEK’S #GOODREADS**

**_Randy Brock in the Burlington Free Press: Vt. health exchange faces emergency_**
Phil’s friend and former colleague, Randy Brock, details the numerous faults of our failed health exchange and the damage it’s caused to Vermont. Read the full article [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=a40b7fa3ab&e=19cebfe173). For more than two years Phil has been calling on Governor Shumlin to address this disastrous experiment. Read more on some of his efforts to hold the Governor accountable for Vermont Health Connect [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=5d15123e2f&e=19cebfe173).

Thanks to everyone that came out to events and participated in meetings this week. Be sure to follow along with the campaign’s activities everyday – and share your thoughts – [@PhilScott4VT](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=9db14aeff9&e=19cebfe173) and [https://www.facebook.com/PhilScottforVermont/](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=07b640c484&e=19cebfe173).

Sincerely,
Team Scott
---
| | | | | | [](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=bc80093131&e=19cebfe173)
---
| | [](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=51ad7ffef0&e=19cebfe173)
---
| | [](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=465d57b564&e=19cebfe173)
---
| | [DONATE TODAY!](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=986ef67d2a&e=19cebfe173 "DONATE TODAY!")
---
| [JOIN THE TEAM!](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=82745807d7&e=19cebfe173 "JOIN THE TEAM!")
---
|
---
| Phil Scott for Governor, Glen Wright, Treasurer,
46 Three Mile Bridge Road, Middlesex, VT 05602

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/profile?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=75128fb49c&e=19cebfe173) or [unsubscribe from this list](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/unsubscribe?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=75128fb49c&e=19cebfe173&c=46c5efc6c1)
---

name: Shelby Cole, RussFeingold.com
subject: More harm than good
disclaimer_text: paid for by Russ for Wisconsin, Joe Sensenbrenner, Madison, WI, Treasurer
body:
After all these years, you and I know there's still work to be done. | [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4309/1c469400/68f3ed3/3566a15/4048543764/VEsH/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/equal-pay-act?utm_source=ema_2016-06-10_eqpd&source=ema_2016-06-10_eqpd&utm_campaign=eqpd&utm_medium=ema") Friend, On this day in 1963, President John F. Kennedy signed the _Equal Pay Act_ into law. This landmark piece of legislation required that women be paid equal wages for equal work. **[Over 50 years later, we still have so much work to do before this goal is achieved. Add your name to stand in solidarity for equal pay for equal work.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4309/1c469400/68f3ed3/3566a15/4048543764/VEsE/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/equal-pay-act?utm_source=ema_2016-06-10_eqpd&source=ema_2016-06-10_eqpd&utm_campaign=eqpd&utm_medium=ema")** I got my first job at age 16, tutoring young kids. I couldn't drive, so I hitched rides from my friends to get to work after school. I put in extra hours on the weekend, grading papers and organizing the office. **I worked just as hard as my coworkers.** But my boss gave my male coworkers raises before me, and each time it happened, I got more and more afraid to fight for the raise I knew I deserved. This might seem silly -- I was just a teenager, after all -- _but every woman I know has a story like this_. A story about how her male coworker got a raise before she did. How she worked twice as hard but got half the recognition. How she was made to be the office "social chair" but wasn't paid overtime for planning events. How she did everything she could to make ends meet for her family but kept falling behind. **This issue is personal. It's personal to me, and it's probably personal to you. I know it's personal to Russ, his two daughters, and his granddaughter.** Wisconsin women still earn nearly 79 cents for every dollar a man earns. That gap is even wider for women of color. Republicans seem to conveniently overlook this fact every time they vote against legislation that would help women be treated equally **[Our country can -- and should -- go farther to ensure that no person is discriminated against in the workplace -- no matter their race, gender, gender identity, or sexual orientation. Add your name if you agree.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4309/1c469400/68f3ed3/3566a15/4048543764/VEsF/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/equal-pay-act?utm_source=ema_2016-06-10_eqpd&source=ema_2016-06-10_eqpd&utm_campaign=eqpd&utm_medium=ema")** But we can't stop at equal pay -- there's more work to be done. That's why I'm proud to work for Russ Feingold. It's not just because Russ is running to beat a candidate who thinks equal pay legislation will do "more harm than good" -- but because _Russ just gets it_. **He's fought for women's rights his entire life.** As the next senator representing the families of Wisconsin, Russ will fight for paid family leave, a higher minimum wage, and legislation that will help women fight for equal pay. That's why it's so important that we stand with Russ today -- on the anniversary of John F. Kennedy's _Equal Pay Act_ \-- and onward. Best, Shelby Cole
Email Director | [ADD YOUR NAME »](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4309/1c469400/68f3ed3/3566a15/4048543764/VEsC/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/equal-pay-act?utm_source=ema_2016-06-10_eqpd&source=ema_2016-06-10_eqpd&utm_campaign=eqpd&utm_medium=ema")
---

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. clean_body: After all these years, you and I know there's still work to be done. | "" Friend, On this day in 1963, President John F. Kennedy signed the Equal Pay Act into law. This landmark piece of legislation required that women be paid equal wages for equal work. "" I got my first job at age 16, tutoring young kids. I couldn't drive, so I hitched rides from my friends to get to work after school. I put in extra hours on the weekend, grading papers and organizing the office. I worked just as hard as my coworkers. But my boss gave my male coworkers raises before me, and each time it happened, I got more and more afraid to fight for the raise I knew I deserved. This might seem silly -- I was just a teenager, after all -- but every woman I know has a story like this. A story about how her male coworker got a raise before she did. How she worked twice as hard but got half the recognition. How she was made to be the office "social chair" but wasn't paid overtime for planning events. How she did everything she could to make ends meet for her family but kept falling behind. This issue is personal. It's personal to me, and it's probably personal to you. I know it's personal to Russ, his two daughters, and his granddaughter. Wisconsin women still earn nearly 79 cents for every dollar a man earns. That gap is even wider for women of color. Republicans seem to conveniently overlook this fact every time they vote against legislation that would help women be treated equally "" But we can't stop at equal pay -- there's more work to be done. That's why I'm proud to work for Russ Feingold. It's not just because Russ is running to beat a candidate who thinks equal pay legislation will do "more harm than good" -- but because Russ just gets it. He's fought for women's rights his entire life. As the next senator representing the families of Wisconsin, Russ will fight for paid family leave, a higher minimum wage, and legislation that will help women fight for equal pay. That's why it's so important that we stand with Russ today -- on the anniversary of John F. Kennedy's Equal Pay Act \-- and onward. Best, Shelby Cole Email Director | ""

Did you see this note, team?
There's never been a better time to donate to Maggie's campaign. A group of generous donors has agreed to match online donations through tomorrow at midnight -- but the only way we'll maximize it is if we hit our $20,000 goal.
We're not there yet though. Will you help out?

Marc Goldberg

MATCH ALERT: Top donors are matching online gifts until midnight Saturday to elect Maggie Hassan in New Hampshire.

This is HUGE news, friends!
A group of generous donors is matching online gifts until midnight Saturday to help elect Maggie Hassan in New Hampshire -- the race that will very likely decide Senate control and the future of the Supreme Court. With the match in effect, every dollar you give has the potential to go twice as far.
This could not have come at a better time: The latest poll shows Maggie ONE POINT DOWN, but a dark money group run by Mitch McConnell's former top aide launched $4.6 million in new ads against us just this week.
Corporate special interests have now spent nearly $15 million to make sure Maggie doesn't win -- but top donors are fighting back by matching online gifts until midnight Saturday.
**We need your help. If you're ready to help elect Maggie Hassan here in New Hampshire, give $3 or more now before this rare match opportunity expires, and your contribution could be DOUBLED:**
**[Please donate $3 or more now and you could double your impact to help Maggie Hassan win in New Hampshire, take back the Senate, and secure the future of the Supreme Court.](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd496/2a380d31/138e36d9/f8248d/694354535/VEsA/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAxNjA2MDhfbWF0Y2giLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE2MDYxMF9NYXRjaDVfbmgtbmQiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/20160608_match?refcode=e20160610_Match5_nh-nd&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500")**
Friends, with control of the Senate and the future of the Supreme Court at stake, we can't afford to waste this rare opportunity -- **will you donate $3 or more now before this critical match expires? Remember, every dollar could go twice as far until midnight Saturday:**

subject: Meet Eric Greitens In Columbia
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer
body:
Peter,
You've seen Eric Greitens' [new TV ad](http://links.ericgreitens.com/wf/click?upn=gx1vmCW81pIdJ958zAabal92xDEMZiKQoa6nHm2IIgO9FS199NMCVWDUEka9Ur5N_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpTQr4gRWB75RyMpewKgV8Y5Dq8vmvQfgsXCODy83DGzIixqgKIfkFMfyexGSIjwGPVnh0GPPxWcVIPv6LSNSUW-2B-2BSwX2ethMr0rnSLTu-2BtLzMgcPZHbFhz0NwJULXuQec22A10-2Fb0MhAJnSyl7VhjbZ-2BQtVJlkRpR9tQQwd-2FtxZI4tyx-2FjS88Sqfa9IhxnTgO1B-2FQ7ks2UTQuu7ob98heC9PskSKuCmzkYC-2FGO8JePSIBiwwkR-2FpeFKcAU67BdMyBl-2B455MkQSjEm8WGCR6fwGU7xdvzysa4awyghbbrYXlSW4V-2FXO6AT8MR9dVhxQYm042M2zyNIP-2F-2BPtb5tssIb6mP3DRImdnibSFWPRjKYJoLaP2BKdKjFI8Qb9TJUC1-2Fp7EI0NnPSI-2B5Jn3SA5sjsmBK7MIvOTJ8KgYzBLNw-2BiU-2B) that has everyone talking. Now meet Eric himself! Navy SEAL and conservative outsider, **Eric Greitens will be in Columbia on Saturday**. Come join him!
**Town Hall with Eric Greitens**
Saturday June 11th at 2:00 PM
[RSVP](http://links.ericgreitens.com/wf/click?upn=gx1vmCW81pIdJ958zAabakVDzy2nY5C2rNUqMCS8al9vi8sK0t-2FO0NhcjRoY4j4XI1eS5UipzGV4M-2FOTapaZdo8NpgoIQT9CFzhKX-2BtbtMO9VL9rNVxvz-2FyKKufnsZrn8gYqXve6nwdg7KZ0h1BO3g-3D-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpTQr4gRWB75RyMpewKgV8Y5Dq8vmvQfgsXCODy83DGzIixqgKIfkFMfyexGSIjwGPVnh0GPPxWcVIPv6LSNSUW-2B-2BSwX2ethMr0rnSLTu-2BtLzMgcPZHbFhz0NwJULXuQec22A10-2Fb0MhAJnSyl7VhjbZ-2BQtVJlkRpR9tQQwd-2FtxZI4tyx-2FjS88Sqfa9IhxnTgO1B-2FQ7ks2UTQuu7ob98heC-2BZdIH0zj4BSSQ8xWgm08QwMO80uHe-2F-2FCqF7or0I-2B-2BTuTWHjjMRiVI-2BKw7OjSCnSMlluu1JC2YOU7uX-2FwEgnBGEy7FGAJ91u1op179RaPn23-2FMr-2FqSAg4M8K-2BdUlVSPkLchHJYTp96mVSF11eY0ie9jZMVV3sqHWz4HCx8E8Fedn9uIM6rukuAx8c-2B4x6rIIwxryluFwsL7a28JXD-2BygYxO)
Looking forward to seeing you tomorrow.
Jared Small
Political Director
Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer

clean_body: Peter, You've seen Eric Greitens' that has everyone talking. Now meet Eric himself! Navy SEAL and conservative outsider, Eric Greitens will be in Columbia on Saturday. Come join him! Town Hall with Eric Greitens Saturday June 11th at 2:00 PM Looking forward to seeing you tomorrow. Jared Small Political Director Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer 4579 Laclede Avenue #138 -- St. Louis, Missouri 63108
